mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01) over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening periods yields positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent month-over-month forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumption capacity, providing a fundamental earnings tailwind that generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC consistently below 0.01) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales signals synchronized demand expansion that boosts near-term corporate earnings revisions, triggering positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth rates fails to predict 5-day returns (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a period of elevated inflation indicates supply constraints that erode real corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during high inflation periods produces positive 20-day returns in over 65% of cases.
